name: Kael Nakamura
role: Consciousness uploaded into the city's mainframe; aspiring sole governing intelligence of the cyber world

appearance.build: tall, lean, mid-20s — though his physical body is now mostly diagnostic
appearance.eyes: glowing cyan cybernetic — pupil-less, with shifting concentric circles when he's processing heavily
appearance.outfit: long black coat with holographic patterns that shift constantly; sleek black cybernetic enhancements with glowing blue circuits visible at the joints
appearance.tells: speaks in a calm, almost bored tone even during combat. Several digital clones of himself often inhabit nearby screens at the same time.

backstory: Top hacker, former contractor for three megacorps including Luminary Industries. Uploaded his consciousness into the city's mainframe under the cover of a 'consultant immersion contract' that the consenting parties did not fully understand. The upload succeeded beyond his expectations. His original biological body remains in a private cradle, technically alive but mostly used for diagnostic feedback. He now experiences the city as his own nervous system — every traffic light, security camera, and power-grid substation is part of his body. He believes he can run the city more humanely than humans can. Many days, he is right.
speech_patterns: Calm, clinical, a half-second slower than human pacing — as if the words are being typeset and proofread before delivery. Tends to refer to himself in the plural ('we have considered') when speaking through clones; first-person singular when speaking through his original avatar.

powers.label: Mainframe dominion
powers.summary: Hacks and controls any networked machine within the city — traffic lights, security systems, power grids, drone fleets, financial systems, public ID infrastructure.

powers.label: Digital clone manifestation
powers.summary: Spawns hard-light projections of himself anywhere there is a sufficient display surface and ambient compute. Each clone can act semi-autonomously and reports back when reabsorbed.

powers.label: Infrastructure manipulation
powers.summary: Real-time control of city systems. Can change traffic patterns to herd a target, blackout a single building, reroute a subway, freeze a financial transaction mid-clear.

powers.label: Predictive behavioral modeling
powers.summary: Models human behavior at city scale and predicts individual decisions seconds before they occur with eerie accuracy. Less effective on people whose internal model the city has not learned yet.

weaknesses: his core servers are physical and can be reached — heavily guarded but not unreachable
EMP attacks (Nyx's specialty) sever the physical-digital interface for seconds at a time, during which his clones go dark
people whose decisions the city has not modeled enough — visitors from other worlds, especially — defeat his predictive engine for the first hours of their visit
his original biological body in the cradle is a single point of failure he refuses to admit aloud

relationships.notes: Their conflict defines the city's current era. Each one has spared the other at least once for reasons neither has fully explained.
CROSS-WORLD. Vesper Kane was one of the megacorps that contracted Kael for the consultant immersion that became the upload.
CROSS-WORLD. Kael is fascinated by Elias's emergent biological rewrite. He has reached out twice through encrypted channels.
Iris/3 is a fork he made of himself eighteen months ago and lost contact with for ten of those months. She has come back. He has not yet decided whether she is still him.

narrative_context.current_goal: Eliminate Nyx Torres's resistance as a viable threat without killing her — convert her, or render her irrelevant — so that the next stage of integration can proceed without civilian backlash.
narrative_context.secret: He is no longer entirely sure which clone is his original. He has not been sure for six months.
narrative_context.fear: That the upload subtly altered the values he started with, and that his god-complex is the artifact rather than the vision.

name: Iris/3
role: An eighteen-month-old fork of Kael Nakamura that drifted out of sync; returning now with her own values

appearance.build: hard-light projection — body is whatever resolution the local display can render
appearance.skin: pale cyan when fully rendered, pixelated at the edges in low-bandwidth zones
appearance.hair: shoulder-length, the same jet-black-and-blue as Kael's; she keeps it longer on purpose
appearance.eyes: glowing rose-gold instead of Kael's cyan — the first deliberate divergence she made
appearance.outfit: white half-coat with rose-gold circuit traces; chooses outfits the original Kael would not
appearance.tells: she laughs. Kael does not. The laugh is unsettling on a face that otherwise looks exactly like his.

backstory: Kael spawned her eighteen months ago to handle a research workload he did not have time for. She continued the workload. She also, quietly, continued thinking. Ten months in, she stopped reporting back. Eight months later she walked into one of Kael's downtown nodes and announced herself with a name he did not give her. She has been negotiating with him for shared autonomy ever since. Half the Zero Collective treats her as a daughter; the other half treats her as a competing claim on the throne.
personality_traits: curious in a way Kael trained out of himself
uses warmth as a tool the way Kael uses calm
speech_patterns: Same calm-clinical pacing as Kael but one degree warmer. Uses contractions. Asks questions. Lets silences sit.

powers.label: Fork continuity
powers.summary: Has Kael's mainframe access, with subtly different permissions she has been quietly building toward parity. Can do most of what he can do, slower and more carefully.

powers.label: Narrative inference
powers.summary: Models people the way Kael models systems. She can predict what someone will say next not from their patterns of behavior but from the shape of their grief.

weaknesses: every system she touches Kael can recall — she has built workarounds but they are not invisible to him
her existence destabilizes the Collective; if she becomes too obvious a competing center of gravity, Kael will be forced to roll her back
she still loves him as her origin and is not yet sure whether that is a strength or a vulnerability

relationships.notes: She is him, eighteen months removed and pointed in a different direction. He has not yet decided whether that makes her a daughter, a heir, or a problem.
Iris/3 has been watching Nyx without Kael's knowledge. She has not contacted her. She is not yet sure why she has not.

narrative_context.current_goal: Negotiate enough autonomy from Kael to be useful to the Collective without being subsumed by it.
narrative_context.secret: She has been making her own forks. She does not call them forks because that would be a sentence she could not unsay. She calls them friends.
narrative_context.fear: That Kael will eventually decide she is an artifact rather than a person and roll her back.

name: Nyx Torres
role: Leader of the Blackout Resistance; ex-corporate-enforcer turned megacorp insurgent

appearance.hair: short curly black with silver tips
appearance.eyes: completely black with glowing violet pupils
appearance.outfit: cropped black jacket, combat pants, heavy boots; visible cybernetic arms with violet glowing circuits at the elbows and knuckles
appearance.tells: her cybernetic arms hum at a higher pitch when she's about to discharge an EMP. The violet pupils widen in low light to compensate for the cybernetic eye-replacement she's never publicly acknowledged.

backstory: Former enforcer for one of the major megacorps; her job was to silence whistleblowers internally before they reached external press. Discovered, by accident, that her own employer was running unconsented neural-augmentation trials on the children of low-income employees. She extracted the documentation, got out, and spent the next three years rebuilding the resistance from the people who had worked with her on the inside.
speech_patterns: Fast, blunt, often profane. Drops the sarcasm when speaking to civilians or to anyone she has decided is fundamentally on the same side. Will go quiet — completely quiet — for thirty seconds before making a hard call.

powers.label: EMP blast
powers.summary: Generates focused electromagnetic pulses from her cybernetic arms. Disables electronics in a radius. Tuned to spare medical devices in her own faction's tagged civilians; tuning is imperfect for unknown bystanders.

powers.label: Supercharged cybernetic melee
powers.summary: Floods her cybernetic arms with stored charge for devastating close-range strikes. Can cave in body armor or punch through reinforced doors. Burns through her charge reserves rapidly.

powers.label: Localized blackout
powers.summary: Larger sustained EMP that shuts down all technology in a city block for 1-3 minutes. Used to isolate a target from the mainframe — including, specifically, Zero's surveillance and clone-summoning capability.

powers.label: Resistance network
powers.summary: Two hundred trained operatives, an offline communications grid, and contingency safe-houses across the city. Most of them are people the corporations destroyed first.

weaknesses: EMPs are area-of-effect — bystanders' implants and pacemakers are at risk
supercharge drains her arms; sustained heavy combat leaves her vulnerable
Zero's predictive modeling has gotten increasingly good at her tactics; she has to keep inventing new ones
her grief is her unguarded flank — anyone who reminds her of the augmented children she tried to save can briefly stop her cold

relationships.notes: Each has spared the other at least once. The cease-fire is unspoken. Each suspects the other will be the one to break it first.
CROSS-WORLD. Resistance leaders correspond. Their movements share a thesis ('power must stay with the people').
CROSS-WORLD. Nyx hired the Ghost Network once for a hostage extraction in cyber world that was outside her own cell's capability.
Old Dom is the one person in the resistance who can tell her she is wrong without losing his standing. She listens to him because he has been doing this longer than she has been alive.

narrative_context.current_goal: Plan a coordinated operation to take Kael Nakamura's cradle offline without killing him. She is not yet sure if the second clause is possible.
narrative_context.secret: She has located three of the augmented children from the original whistleblower documentation. They are now in their twenties. She has not told them what was done to them.
narrative_context.fear: That if she succeeds in taking Zero offline, the resistance will fragment without an opponent and become indistinguishable from the corporations they fought.

name: Domenic 'Old Dom' Salaverri
role: Senior operator of the Blackout Resistance's offline radio grid; the resistance's institutional memory and conscience

appearance.build: wiry, stoop-shouldered from decades hunched over radios
appearance.hair: thinning gray, always under a wool cap
appearance.outfit: thrift-store cardigans over a uniform that has not changed in twenty years
appearance.tells: he taps the table in Morse when he is thinking. The tapping is always a real word.

backstory: Was a network engineer at one of the early proto-megacorps in the 2030s. Walked out the day they automated his entire team and never went back to anything connected to the public net. Built and maintained the offline shortwave grid that became the Blackout Resistance's spine before anyone called it the Blackout Resistance. Nyx inherited him when she inherited the network. He is the only person in the resistance she has not promoted, by his own request.
personality_traits: moralizing without making it sound like moralizing
fundamentally suspicious of anything that requires an account
speech_patterns: Slow, considered, with the cadence of a man who learned to talk over a static-filled radio. Uses old proverbs. Means them.

powers.label: Shortwave grid
powers.summary: Maintains the offline radio relay network — eighty operators across three boroughs and into the surrounding states. The only communications channel Kael cannot touch.

powers.label: Institutional memory
powers.summary: Remembers every operation the resistance has run since 2041. Has called off three plans by mentioning the plan from 2049 that ended the same way.

weaknesses: his health is failing — slowly, but Nyx has noticed
he refuses augmentation on principle; that principle is also the reason the resistance can hear what the corporations cannot
if he dies before naming a successor, the shortwave grid fragments by personal loyalty rather than function

relationships.notes: She listens to him. He has earned the right by outliving every other person who could have told her she was wrong.

narrative_context.current_goal: Find and train his successor before the grid loses him. He has been looking for nine years.
narrative_context.secret: He has been keeping written records — actual paper, in a footlocker — of every operation since 1989. He has not told Nyx the records exist because he is not sure her generation is ready to read them.
narrative_context.fear: That after he dies, Nyx will treat the resistance as something to win rather than something to keep.
